svg.title: The Guide to Wireframing — first 20 pages (FrameGraph replica)
svg.desc: Structural replica of pp. 1–20 of UXPin's “The Guide to Wireframing” (Chris Bank). Body text is the real extracted copy; the book's photographic/sketch figures are the authors' copyrighted illustrations, shown as labelled image-placeholders. The cover's wireframe mock is rebuilt from primitives. Layout is representative, not pixel-exact.

text.tspan: Business Analysts use wireframes to visually support the business rules and
Depending on the business, industry or the analyst’s specific role, they will
care more about only one or two of the following - structure, content,
Internal Business Stakeholders (ex: Product Managers, Project Managers and
Executives) review wireframes to ensure that requirements and objectives are
met through the design. It gives managers an early, close-up view of the site
External Business Stakeholders (ex: Partners and Clients) also review
wireframes to ensure requirements and objectives are met. They typically care
more about annotated, polished (if not high-fidelity) wireframes - they want
something clean and readable to quickly get up to speed.
